rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees that reference component ports, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model when writing AGREE contracts.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component types for structural assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Generated AGREE code must not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the analyzed model.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible features and ports at their respective scope levels.
Avoid using implementation-specific subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations: `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`.
Use nested `if ... then ... else` for branching logic in AGREE.
AGREE logical operators include `and`, `or`, `not(...)`, and implication `=>`.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid outputs or assignments.
Do not reference non-visible identifiers or types that are not part of the AADL model in AGREE contracts.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
AGREE annex placement for assume/guarantee syntax
AGREE requirement semantics and compact form
